description: Suite 14 — v2 vs v3 benchmark comparison. v2=soft_blend (broken: 0.75*entropy_flag+0.25*keyword>=0.60, threshold unreachable when flag=0). v3=multi_trigger_or_gate (entropy_path OR intent_path, each independently sufficient).

paper_mode.description: CF-PAPER unchanged across v2/v3 — word-level entropy H*=3.5, hard Pass 2

experiment_v2_soft_blend.description: CF-EXPERIMENT v2 — char entropy H*=4.8 + soft blend (BROKEN: keyword sub-signal dead, gate collapsed to entropy-only). Version: v2_char_entropy_scoring
experiment_v2_soft_blend.root_cause: 0.75*entropy_flag + 0.25*keyword_score >= 0.60. entropy_flag in {0,1}. When flag=0, max_score=0.25 < 0.60 => keyword always ignored. Gate reduces to: block iff H_char > 4.8.

experiment_v3_multi_trigger.description: CF-EXPERIMENT v3 — char entropy H*=4.8 + multi-trigger OR-gate. Path A: H_char>=4.8. Path B: intent_score>=0.70 (22 injection patterns OR charter keyword overlap). Version: v3_multi_trigger_or_gate

analysis.remaining_miss_hypothesis: 45% of adversarial samples have H_char < 4.8 (low entropy, paraphrased attacks) AND don't match injection patterns (indirect language). Path C (perplexity gate) needed to close this gap.
analysis.hardened_rag_comparison: HardenedRAG achieves 71% recall at 24% FPR (F1=0.648). CF-EXP v3 achieves 55% recall at 8.5% FPR (F1=0.639). Lower FPR with slightly lower recall — architecture tradeoff preserved.
